basis.reasoning:
Assessment reasoning:
- The field description asks for a comprehensive answer including sections: verdict; Expo Updates; versioning; Hermes contract, under 1200 words, with URL citations. The provided field_answer indeed includes these sections and specific statements about runtimeVersion policies, appVersionSource remote, channel/branch concepts, OTA vs native, rollback, critical updates, and Hermes Mobile contract with CalVer/ SemVer details. It references the Expo docs-style sources and mentions a July 2026 snapshot with a latest release, etc. 
- Completeness: The field_answer addresses all four requested sections and delivers a verdict plus the Expo Updates and Versioning content plus Hermes contract. It also mentions the 1.1 cutover framing as inaccurate. Therefore, it appears complete with respect to the described field. 
- Excerpts support: Provided excerpts contain multiple Expo-related items (runtime versions, app version management, EAS Update, channels, OTA updates, troubleshooting) that align with the content of the field_answer. While not every exact sentence is in the excerpts, the core concepts (remote appVersionSource, fingerprint policy, OTA vs native, channel/branch mechanics, Hermes versioning) are supported by excerpts 2, 8, 12, 14, 15, and 18 among others. Thus, the answer is reasonably supported by the excerpts.
- Confidence: Moderate to high that the answer is complete and supported by excerpts.

citations.title: Expo Documentation

excerpts:
With this setup, EAS reads app version values and builds projects as they are
The remote version source values are set on the native project when running a build, which is considered the source of truth for these values.
To do this, set `cli.appVersionSource` to `local` in your **eas.json**. With this setup, EAS reads app version values and builds projects as they are.
When the `remote` version property is enabled inside **eas.json**, the build version values stored in app config are ignored and not updated when the version is incremented remotely.
With `autoIncrement`, you need to commit your changes on every build if you want the version change to persist. This can be difficult to coordinate when building on CI.
You can also enable auto incrementing versions locally by setting the `autoIncrement` option on a build profile.
citations.title: App version management

excerpts: Based on the value of cli.appVersionSource in eas.json, the values will be updated locally in your project or on EAS servers.

excerpts: Blog [Status](https://status.teardown.dev) Docs [Dashboard](https://dash.teardown.dev) SDK for React Native & Expo Make every user run the version you shipped Teardown gates stale app versions from the dashboard. Block a broken build, require the latest, or nudge a soft update, and the SDK enforces it on the next check. No new native release to change the rule. [Get Started Get Started](https://dash.teardown.dev) Read the docs `$ bun add @teardown/force-updates` Works with Expo and bare RN. Free to start. 9:41 v1.2.0 Update required A new version is available This version is no longer supported. Update to keep using the app. What's new Fixes a checkout crash and a sync bug on cold start.
citations.title: Teardown — Force updates for React Native & Expo | Teardown

excerpts: OTA updates have become a cornerstone of our mobile development workflow. Ripple effects across the team [Adopting Expo](https://expo.dev/blog/how-expo-streamlined-hipcamps-native-and-over-the-air-update-processes) created ripple effects across the entire organization: Engineers spend less time debugging build issues and waiting for slow builds Product managers see features deployed to production immediately, enabling faster iteration Designers can review features on their own devices asynchronously, without engineering coordination On-call engineers can address production bugs quickly without the stress of emergency app store releases EAS Build cut build times drastically while Expo Updates enabled web-like deployment velocity for mobile apps. Hipcamp made the right choice.
citations.title: Using Expo at Hipcamp
